title: The Floof-Berry Mission and the Great Snowstorm
summary: Seeker and Disco set off on a magical journey to Izzy's attic, using a special potion and a magic rhyme to transport themselves there. Their mission is to find parts for a machine that spins fast enough to fly like a helicopter, allowing Seeker to deliver essential floof-berries to the inhabitants of the distant Upper Floofer Cloud. While Seeker focuses on his aerial delivery, Disco plans to build a reinforced mole tunnel to protect them from a massive approaching snowstorm. As Bunsen tracks the howling winds and deep snow on a specialized storm tracker, the group prepares to stay cozy indoors, growing seeds for the cats and waiting for the sun to return so they can eventually play in the deep drifts.

transcript.text: It's just for at night.

Tell me a story about Seeker goes to the attic with Disco.

transcript.text: Seeker goes to the attic with Disco. Well, once upon a time...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Izzy's attic!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Seeker... Ding-dong! "Oh, hello," said Seeker, "who is it?"
It's Disco.

Dad
transcript.text: "Hi Disco, come on in."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "I just heard that we can explore Izzy's attic. Do you have an Izzy's house potion?"
"Well, I think I do. Let me rummage around in my potion closet. Let's see..."

Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I want to be right in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, yes. Get right in my lap. That's just right. All right, oh here's a Planet of the Mookers potion, a Floofer Cloud potion, what else have we got here... oh, the Muck and Yuck World potion. Ah, here it is, Izzy's Attic potion. And why do you need to go there, Disco?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Cause it's a fun place."

"I think so too, but you know, I was working on something that really requires me to have a little machine that can spin really fast. Do you think the attic is a place where I could find some parts to help me build that, Seeker?"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "Yes, there's definitely some Legos and a kit with all kinds of interesting parts. Hey, Izza Ka Pizza, that's the rhyme today."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. So Seeker and Disco sipped the potion... and they said the rhyme.
"Izza Ka Pizza. Izza Ka Pizza. Izza Ka Pizza."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And then... poof! They reappeared in Izzy's attic. But...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And then Izzy said, "Hey, I observed you go a kit with a spinning wheel in it and an LED and really lose-able parts."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Really lose-able parts? Oh no! Well, remember to put them in the kit when you're done, okay? Here's your whole kit. It also has a red spinning thing inside and an instruction book. Oh, and also you can do whatever you want with the parts."
"Well, thank you. Now want to have a peep inside? I'll take out the instruction book for you so you can look at it. Dad, remember the things we used with the kit with the red wheel? Remember?"
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Was that the Snap Circuits kit that also has the sound-making thing?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, the Snap Circuits, Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, Snap Circuits. Very interesting. And this red wheel, can it be made to spin?"
"Yeah."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "And can it spin very fast?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Really fast, and then it can go up in the air and then land on your hand."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "It can do all of that? Astonishing," said Seeker. "Well, that's lucky for me because that's exactly what I need. I need to try to make something spin so fast it can fly up in the air. That's what..."
"Yeah, and then it's supposed to land on your hand. That's what it does."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "I see. But what I need to do is use it to carry some floof-berries high up in the air. You see, because that's why I need something that could spin so fast so it becomes like a helicopter and it flies up to deliver floof-berries to the Floofers that live on the upper Floofer Cloud. I don't know if you knew this, but there's not just one Floofer Cloud, there's many. And right now the Floofers have a problem. They need to deliver their floof-berries up high up to the higher floof clouds, but they can't. So I said I would try to build them a machine, and maybe this thing can help. What do you think?"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "Yeah. And also I observed you this Lego kit."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Oh, thank you, yes. Because we're going to need to find some way to hold the floof-berries and attach them to the spinning flying disk. Hmm, maybe we can use Legos to make a little basket."
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "Yes. And Disco, what would you like to build?"
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "I would like to build a mole tunnel!"
"Oh, for us to keep warm in the storm with a door that has 100 lockers on it."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, 100 lockers? Why does it need that? You mean locks?"
"To help the snow not to stay outside and never ever come in."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Oh, yes."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Cause I want to be warm. That's why I need 100 lockers."
"I see. And what else is your tunnel going to have?"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "Everything that's impossible for a big lights-off storm."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Oh, is a storm coming?" said Seeker. "I hadn't heard about a storm."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Yeah, Dad. Remember what... and maybe the electricity will go out and then the dishwasher won't be working, so we'll have to use the sink."
"Oh, I see," said Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "To wash our dishes. It's very important to be prepared for a big storm. And we also need a flashlight and warm clothes. That's true, yeah. And the snow is going to be really deep."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, that's actually kind of exciting," said Seeker. "Maybe we can dig some snow tunnels."
"Oh no, no, no, we can't."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Why not?"
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "We can't go out in the snow at all, we're not."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Why not?"
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Because it will be completely so dark and also so snowy and it will be too deep."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh. Well, what could we do in the snow that would be fun?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Oh, I'm sorry, we can't. We'll have to stay inside."
"Oh. I hope we get to go out and play once it stops snowing, at least," said Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Oh, it won't stop snowing anytime soon."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, it will. The snow won't last all that long. I think after it stops snowing, it's going to be bright and sunny and there'll be deep snow everywhere and kids outside playing and building snowmen and making snow angels and sledding and everything."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Yeah. And maybe we can start some seeds, but just inside. And then each day we can bring them outside to get some sun to grow."
Oh, that's a good idea.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "That's what we can do. That's what we can do to make indoor plants for the cats."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "For the cats, I see. And what kind of a machine could we build to help us with the snow, do you think? Let's ask Bunsen... Bunsen!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "Oh, yes! Hello, who is it?" "It's Seeker and Disco. And also they need a fun snow machine."
"Oh. Hmm."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "We were talking about the storm that's going to happen."
"Yes, I have been watching the weather forecasts carefully. It does appear that a major storm is coming soon with high winds and we'll get some deep, deep snow."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Hey, this is my time wind tracker, of... it helps me know when a very deep snowstorm's coming and the lights will go out."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, I see."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: "I keep it on the side of my bed in case I wake up in the night and I want to watch the vision of the snow and see if the high wind is howling. The high wind is howling!"
"We might hear some high wind howling during the snowstorm, that's true, but we'll be safe inside," said Bunsen.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "And I might have to get prepared with some headphones."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, you might, you might not."
"I think the howling wind will blow and also I may shake a little and shiver a little."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "Oh, but I think we'll all be nice and warm the whole time."
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: "Well, also we may also need headphones for the wind."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Okay, well we have those just in case," said Bunsen. "We're very well prepared."
"Yeah, I've got my time storm tracker."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Yes, that helps you know when the storm is coming. And how soon is the storm coming?"
"I don't know, it's just short-nap long-nap storm."
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: "Oh, I think it's long-nap short-nap storm, but yes," said Bunsen. "It's coming tomorrow night and then the following day most likely school will be canceled so you'll be home and it will still be snowing, but we'll all play inside the house."
"Yeah, and maybe I'll also get a chance to rest."
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: "I think that would be very nice, that's a good idea. We'll snuggle up and be cozy inside the house and just do what we need to do to be happy and comfortable and cozy."
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: "Yeah, and even prepared with our headphones on. I'll have them right outside my bed in this tray in case I need to wear them in the night."
"Yeah." All right, I think that's the end of our stars and stories time.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Wait, wait! I want shadow puppets!
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Bunsen
characters.description: An obsessive chronometeorologist who views sleep as a luxury the world cannot afford, they spend their days and sunless nights tethered to a brass-bound "time wind tracker." With eyes bloodshot from perpetual vigilance, they monitor the shifting currents of temporal drafts, sacrificing rest to calibrate their instruments and fortify their laboratory against the encroaching gale of an impending storm.

characters.name: The Floofers
characters.description: Tiny, pom-pom-like, and irrepressibly bouncy, these magical ball-shaped denizens of the Floofer Clouds leap with wide-eyed grace across resilient, vaporous peaks. Sustained by a vital dependency on floof-berries, these restless creatures possess a staggering verticality and a vibrant energy so potent that falling asleep remains an elusive feat.

characters.name: The Cats
characters.description: Dust-mantled creatures who shed fine sediment across the floors for the harvesting moles, awaiting the arrival of indoor greenery cultivated through the long winter snowstorms.

places.name: Izzy's Attic
places.description: Accessible only to those who swallow a shimmering potion and recite the ancient rhyme, this hidden workshop is a sanctuary of infinite creation. Within, sprawling Lego landscapes and intricate kits merge with a vibrant chaos of art supplies and heavy wooden work desks. Weathered suitcases, brimming with mystery, rest among the colorful bricks, marking a space where every tool and trinket is a portal to a new imagination.

places.name: Izzy's House
places.description: Once the silent harbor of Jinjar’s return and the site of Rizzo and Izzy’s fateful council, this fortified sanctuary now serves as the primary destination reachable via a magical potion. Anchoring the company against the encroaching storm, the hallowed dwelling resembles a quaint hotel of labyrinthine rooms, featuring a celestial starry rug that blankets the ground where heroes once prepared for their departure to the dragon’s castle.

places.name: Upper Floofer Cloud
places.description: The high-altitude destination where Seeker must deliver floof-berries using a flying machine.

places.name: Mole Tunnel
places.description: A warm, secure underground passage Disco plans to build, featuring 100 locks to keep out the snow.

places.name: Planet of the Mookers
places.description: An enigmatic alien world defined by its fuzzy yet curiously slimy surface, this planet serves as the ancestral cradle of the Mookers and the site of Chicky’s destined path. Strewn with the colossal remains of giant fossils, the realm was recently scouted by Seeker and Buddy and documented in the Seeker’s potion closet; it remains accessible only to those who imbibe a specific purple alchemical elixir.

places.name: Muck
places.description: A messy destination accessible via a specific potion.

places.name: Yuck World
places.description: A messy destination accessible via a specific potion.
